perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the agreement duration from two years to one year creates a contradiction with the introductory paragraph that implicitly assumes a longer-term relationship based on ongoing discussions and sharing of confidential information. It introduces an in-text contradiction because a one-year term might be insufficient for the discussions and projects initially contemplated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS Roundhouse Creative P/L a n d ……………………………………. ( t h e “ P a r t i e s ” ) h a v e a n interest in participating in discussions wherein either Party might share information with the other that the disclosing Party considers to be proprietary and confidential to itself (“Confidential Information”); and WHEREAS the Parties agree that Confidential Information of a Party might include, but not be limited to that Party’s: (1) business plans, methods, and practices; (2) personnel, customers, and suppliers; (3) inventions, processes, methods, products, patent applications, and other proprietary rights; or (4) specifications, drawings, sketches, models, samples, tools, computer programs, technical information, or other related information;
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement duration from two years to one year. The contradicted text indicates that the parties anticipate ongoing discussions and sharing of confidential information, suggesting a relationship longer than one year. Therefore, reducing the agreement duration creates an in-text contradiction because the reduced timeframe may be insufficient for the activities contemplated in the contradicted section. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because the changed text provides a conflicting deadline, which leads to disputes over contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential either by marking it, in the case of written materials, or, in the case of information that is disclosed orally or written materials that are not marked, by notifying the other Party of the proprietary a n d confidential nature of the information, such notification to be done orally, by e-mail or written correspondence, or via other means of communication as might be appropriate.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may disclose Confidential Information to the other Party in confidence provided that the disclosing Party identifies such information as proprietary and confidential by marking it in the case of written materials. Information disclosed orally or in unmarked written materials shall not be considered Confidential Information under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change restricts the definition of Confidential Information, excluding orally disclosed or unmarked written material from protection. This directly contradicts the original agreement, which allowed for oral notification of confidentiality. The change creates an in-text contradiction by negating the initial broader definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS Roundhouse Creative P/L a n d ……………………………………. ( t h e “ P a r t i e s ” ) h a v e a n interest in participating in discussions wherein either Party might share information with the other that the disclosing Party considers to be proprietary and confidential to itself (“Confidential Information”); and WHEREAS the Parties agree that Confidential Information of a Party might include, but not be limited to that Party’s: (1) business plans, methods, and practices; (2) personnel, customers, and suppliers; (3) inventions, processes, methods, products, patent applications, and other proprietary rights; or (4) specifications, drawings, sketches, models, samples, tools, computer programs, technical information, or other related information;
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' by excluding orally disclosed or unmarked written materials. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that the parties might share information that the disclosing party considers proprietary and confidential, and that Confidential Information might include business plans, methods, practices, inventions, processes, etc., without restricting the form of disclosure (oral or written, marked or unmarked). The original agreement also allowed for oral notification to ensure confidentiality. The change creates a clear and direct conflict with the initial broader definition of Confidential Information and how it may be disclosed, fulfilling the conditions of an Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient is encouraged to return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information, but is not required to do so.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall honor' to 'is encouraged' transforms a mandatory obligation into a suggestion. This introduces an in-text contradiction with the earlier sections which assume mandatory compliance for protecting confidential information and implies a discretionary approach which undermines the initial agreement about protecting confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Non-Disclosure Agreement -Page 2 of 3- CONFIDENTIAL Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'The Recipient shall honor any request...to promptly return or destroy all copies...', which creates a mandatory obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'The Recipient is encouraged to return or destroy all copies... but is not required to do so,' changing the obligation to a suggestion. The contradicted text explicitly states, 'The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies...'. The changed text directly contradicts this pre-existing mandatory obligation in the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction because what was once required is now optional.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall ensure that each of its employees, officers, directors, or agents who has access to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement is informed of its proprietary and confidential nature and is required to abide by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall use reasonable efforts to ensure that its employees, officers, directors, or agents who have direct access to Confidential Information are generally aware of its confidential nature. They are not strictly required to abide by all terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Weakening the obligation to 'reasonable efforts' and removing the strict requirement to abide by all terms creates an in-text contradiction. The original agreement places a firm responsibility on the recipient. Changing the 'Recipient shall ensure' to 'reasonable efforts to ensure' creates an uncertainty on how much or how hard the Recipient will take action to abide by the agreement which contradicts the intention of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: When informed of the proprietary and confidential nature of Confidential Information that has been disclosed by the other Party, the receiving Party (“Recipient”) shall, for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, refrain from disclosing such Confidential Information to any contractor or other third party without prior, written approval from the disclosing Party and shall protect such Confidential Information from inadvertent disclosure to a third party using the same care and diligence that the Recipient uses to protect its own proprietary and confidential information, but in no case less than reasonable care. The Recipient shall ensure that each of its employees, officers, directors, or agents who has access to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement is informed of its proprietary and confidential nature and is required to abide by the terms of this Agreement. The Recipient of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall promptly notify the disclosing Party of any disclosure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement or of any subpoena or other legal process requiring production or disclosure of said Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Recipient *shall ensure* that employees abide by the terms of the agreement. The changed text weakens this obligation to *reasonable efforts* and removes the strict requirement to abide by *all terms*. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states 'The Recipient shall ensure that each of its employees, officers, directors, or agents who has access to Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement is informed of its proprietary and confidential nature and is required to abide by the terms of this Agreement.' The change introduces uncertainty about the extent to which the Recipient will enforce the agreement among its employees, creating a strong in-text contradiction, which makes it an instance of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Non-Disclosure Agreement -Page 2 of 3- CONFIDENTIAL Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: While the Parties acknowledge that the disclosing Party might suffer injury if its Confidential Information is disclosed in breach of this Agreement, the availability of injunctive relief and the award of damages will be at the sole discretion of the court.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'at the sole discretion of the court' introduces uncertainty and contradicts the original clause that guarantees the disclosing party the right to injunctive relief and damages. This creates an in-text contradiction by changing the legal remedy from a guaranteed right to a possibility depending on the court's decision. This affects the strength and reliability of the legal protection promised in the original agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Non-Disclosure Agreement -Page 2 of 3- CONFIDENTIAL Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that the disclosing party 'shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief... and an award of actual and exemplary damages'. The changed text modifies this by stating that the availability of injunctive relief and damages will be 'at the sole discretion of the court'. This directly contradicts the original guarantee of relief and damages, making it conditional and uncertain. The contradicted text relies on the original guarantee to ensure the protection of confidential information, and the changed text undermines this protection.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Party will, without prior approval of the other Party, make any public announcement of or otherwise disclose the existence or the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may disclose the existence of this agreement, but not its specific terms, unless required by law.
perturbation.explanation: This modification allows either party to disclose the existence of the agreement without the other party's consent, but forbids disclosing the specific terms. This intext contradicts the initial clause that prohibits any public announcement or disclosure without approval from the other party. It undermines the privacy and confidentiality initially intended by the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither Party will, without prior approval of the other Party, make any public announcement of or otherwise disclose the existence or the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text permits disclosure of the agreement's existence, while the contradicted text prohibits any public announcement or disclosure without prior approval. This creates a direct conflict, as the changed text allows an action (disclosure of existence) that the contradicted text forbids unless approved by the other party, fulfilling the definition of an inconsistency causing an in-text contradiction.
